intro:
How to use this course (A2)

This is the A2 (Bàsic) course — it continues straight on from A1. We assume you can already read Catalan aloud from the IPA, use the present tense, and handle greetings, numbers and basic descriptions. A2 adds the past tenses, the future and conditional, weak and combined pronouns, and the vocabulary and functions you need to narrate, describe and give opinions — and to pass the official Certificat de nivell bàsic (A2).
ipaGuide:
Quick IPA refresher: A2 assumes the A1 sound system. The essentials below are all you need on this page; the full guide lives in the A1 course.

Vowels

Central Catalan has eight vowels. The one that catches everyone: unstressed a and e both become the schwa /ə/, and unstressed o becomes /u/. Stressed e/o are open /ɛ ɔ/ or closed /e o/.

Consonants & habits — the golden rule

Written v = /b/; b, d, g soften between vowels /β ð ɣ/; the final -r of infinitives is silent (parlar /pəɾˈla/); x = /ʃ/, ll = /ʎ/, ny = /ɲ/.

Resources — pronunciation Refresh: the A1 IPA guide in this app · interactive chart → jbdowse.com/ipa
ipaCheat:
Vowels

Central Catalan has eight vowels. The one that catches everyone: unstressed a and e both become the schwa /ə/, and unstressed o becomes /u/. Stressed e/o are open /ɛ ɔ/ or closed /e o/.

Consonants & habits — the golden rule

Written v = /b/; b, d, g soften between vowels /β ð ɣ/; the final -r of infinitives is silent (parlar /pəɾˈla/); x = /ʃ/, ll = /ʎ/, ny = /ɲ/.
examInfo:
Exam preparation

The official A2 exam

What the certificate is

The Certificat de nivell bàsic de català (A2) certifies you can handle everyday social situations in Catalan. It has four areas — communicative appropriateness (15%), listening (30%), reading (25%) and speaking (30%). You pass with at least 60% in speaking and 60% across the rest. This course practises appropriateness inside the units; the mock below times you on listening, reading, writing and speaking.

Where to sit it

Catalonia: the Generalitat (Direcció General de Política Lingüística). Andorra: the Govern d'Andorra runs its own official exams. Worldwide / universities: the Institut Ramon Llull and Intercat coordinate external exams.

Official exam routes Generalitat (A2): structure & description · Andorra: official exams
Institut Ramon Llull: external certificates · Intercat: certificates · Practice course (free): Parla.cat (Bàsic)

cite: Level and exam structure: Generalitat de Catalunya (llengua.gencat.cat) and the CPNL grammar contents (cpnl.cat). Per-unit practice links are third-party resources, free at the time of writing. Catalan content pending native review.
checklist.0: talk about yourself and describe people (looks and character).
checklist.5: tell a simple story, combining the past tenses.
checklist.6: replace objects with weak pronouns (el, la, en, hi).
checklist.14: take part in everyday A2 conversations and pass the nivell bàsic exam.
u1.title: Back into Catalan
u1.block.0: By the end you can: re-activate your Catalan — the present tense, ser vs estar — and use the connectors that turn isolated A1 phrases into real A2 sentences.
u1.block.2: The three conjugations in the present, with the most useful irregulars. You met these at A1; make sure they are automatic before we add the past tenses.
u1.block.3:
Pronounparlar (1st)perdre (2nd)dormir (3rd)
joparloperdodormo
tuparlesperdsdorms
ell/ellaparlaperddorm
nosaltresparlemperdemdormim
vosaltresparleuperdeudormiu
ells/ellesparlenperdendormen
u1.block.4: Many 3rd-conjugation verbs are incoatius and add -eix-: servir → serveixo /səɾˈbɛʃu/ I serve.
u1.block.6:
CatalanIPA (jo-form)English
ser — soc, ets, és, som, sou, són/ˈsɔk/to be (essence)
estar — estic, estàs, està, estem, esteu, estan/əsˈtik/to be (state/place)
tenir — tinc, tens, té, tenim, teniu, tenen/ˈtiŋ/to have
fer — faig, fas, fa, fem, feu, fan/ˈfatʃ/to do/make
anar — vaig, vas, va, anem, aneu, van/ˈbatʃ/to go
haver — he, has, ha, hem, heu, han/ˈe/to have (auxiliary)
u1.block.8: Use ser for identity, origin, profession and permanent traits (Soc de Lleida I'm from Lleida); use estar for location, temporary states and how you feel (Estic cansat I'm tired). The auxiliary haver builds the perfet you'll learn in Unit 3.
u1.block.9: Connectors — from A1 phrases to A2 sentences
u1.block.10:
CatalanIPAEnglish
i / o/i/ /o/and / or
però/pəˈɾɔ/but
perquè/pəɾˈkɛ/because
també / tampoc/təmˈbe/ /təmˈpɔk/also / neither
doncs/ˈdoŋs/so, then
llavors / aleshores/ʎəˈβos/ /ələˈzɔɾəs/then, at that point
primer / després / finalment/pɾiˈme/ /dəsˈpɾes/ /finəlˈmen/first / after / finally
per exemple/pəɾ əɡˈzemplə/for example
és a dir/ˌez ə ˈði/that is, i.e.
u1.block.11:
Es retroben — Catching up

— Hola, Marc! Quant de temps! Com estàs? Hi, Marc! Long time! How are you?

— Molt bé, gràcies. Ara treballo en una llibreria i també estudio català. Very well, thanks. Now I work in a bookshop and I'm also studying Catalan.

— Ah, doncs parles molt bé! I on vius ara? Ah, so you speak really well! And where do you live now?

— Visc a Girona, però soc de Reus. I tu, què fas? I live in Girona, but I'm from Reus. And you, what do you do?

u2.title: People & Descriptions
u2.block.0: By the end you can: describe what people look like and what they're like, and compare them — a staple of the A2 speaking and writing tasks.
u2.block.2: Adjectives agree in gender and number and usually come after the noun: un noi alt a tall boy, una noia alta, uns nois alts, unes noies altes. Most add -a for the feminine; many ending in -e or a consonant like -ent don't change (alegre, intel·ligent).
u2.block.3:
CatalanIPAEnglish (opposite)
alt / baix/ˈal/ /ˈbaʃ/tall / short
prim / gros/ˈpɾim/ /ˈɡɾɔs/thin / fat
jove / gran/ˈʒɔβə/ /ˈɡɾan/young / old
simpàtic / antipàtic/simˈpatik/nice / unpleasant
alegre / seriós/əˈleɣɾə/ /səɾiˈos/cheerful / serious
treballador / mandrós/tɾəβəʎəˈðo/ /mənˈdɾos/hard-working / lazy
tímid / obert/ˈtimit/ /uˈβɛɾt/shy / open
u2.block.5:
els cabells/kəˈβeʎs/hair
ros / morè / castany/ˈros/ /muˈɾɛ/ /kəsˈtaɲ/blond / dark / brown
els ulls blaus / verds / marrons/ˈuʎs/blue / green / brown eyes
u2.block.7: més … que more … than · menys … que less … than · tan … com as … as. Superlative: el/la més …. Irregular: bo → millor good → better, dolent → pitjor bad → worse.
u2.block.8:
Qui és aquell? — Who's that?

— Qui és aquell noi alt dels cabells rossos? Who's that tall boy with blond hair?

— És en Pau. És molt simpàtic, però una mica tímid. That's Pau. He's very nice, but a bit shy.

— I la noia? És més alta que ell. And the girl? She's taller than him.

— És la seva germana. És tan simpàtica com ell. She's his sister. She's as nice as he is.
u2.block.9: Resources for Unit 2 Practise: adjectives & agreement → talkpal.ai · Free course: Parla.cat Bàsic → parla.cat

u3.title: The Recent Past — el perfet
u3.block.0: By the end you can: say what you have done today and recently — the first past tense and a frequent A2 listening/speaking target.
u3.block.2: The perfet = present of haver + the past participle. Use it for the recent past, especially within today or "so far".
u3.block.3:
haver+ participleEnglish
heparlatI have spoken
hasmenjatyou have eaten
habeguthe/she has drunk
hemdormitwe have slept
heuvistyou (pl) have seen
hanfetthey have done
u3.block.5: Regular: -ar → -at (parlat), -re/-er → -ut (perdut, begut), -ir → -it (dormit). Key irregulars: fer → fet, dir → dit, veure → vist, escriure → escrit, obrir → obert, prendre → pres, venir → vingut.
u3.block.6: Time markers that love the perfet: avui today, aquest matí this morning, ja already, encara no not yet, mai (n)ever.
u3.block.7:
Què has fet avui? — What have you done today?

— Què has fet avui? What have you done today?

— He treballat al matí i he dinat amb la Júlia. I tu? I worked in the morning and had lunch with Júlia. And you?

— Encara no he fet res! Però ja he comprat el regal. I haven't done anything yet! But I've already bought the present.
u3.block.8: Resources for Unit 3 Conjugate the perfet: any verb → verbs.cat/en/practise · Drill: past tenses → talkpal.ai
ex.3.1.title: Conjugate haver (the perfet auxiliary).

ex.3.6.i0.html: ___ he comprat el pa, no cal anar a la botiga.
ex.3.6.i1.html: ___ he treballat molt; estic cansat.
ex.3.7.title: Reorder into a correct sentence.

ex.3.9.title: Listen and write the Catalan you hear.

u4.title: Narrating the Past — el passat perifràstic
u4.block.0: By the end you can: narrate finished past events — what you did yesterday, last week, on your trip. This is the everyday "past simple" of Catalan.
u4.block.2: Catalan's most common past for finished events is the passat perifràstic: a special form of anar + the infinitive. (It looks like "to go", but it means "did".)
u4.block.3:
Auxiliary+ infinitiveEnglish
vaigparlarI spoke
vasmenjaryou ate
vabeurehe/she drank
vamdormirwe slept
vauveureyou (pl) saw
vanferthey did
u4.block.4: perfet vs perifràstic: use the perfet for today/recent (avui he menjat); use the perifràstic for finished, dated past (ahir vaig menjar). Markers: ahir yesterday, la setmana passada last week, l'any passat last year, fa dos dies two days ago.
u4.block.5:
El cap de setmana — The weekend

— Què vas fer el cap de setmana? What did you do at the weekend?

— Vaig anar a Girona i vaig visitar la catedral. I vosaltres? I went to Girona and visited the cathedral. And you?

— Vam quedar a casa i vam veure una pel·lícula. We stayed home and watched a film.
u4.block.6: Resources for Unit 4 Conjugate the perifràstic: → verbs.cat/en/practise · Drill past tenses: → talkpal.ai

ex.4.4.i1.html: "vaig menjar" means "I am going to eat".
ex.4.4.i2.html: You use it for finished, dated past events.

u5.title: The Way Things Were — l'imperfet
u5.block.0: By the end you can: describe past habits, settings and how things used to be — "when I was little", "we always used to…".
u5.block.2: Regular endings: -ar → -ava (parlava), and -re/-er/-ir → -ia (perdia, dormia). Use it for habits, repeated actions and description in the past (age, weather, feelings, scenes).
u5.block.3:
Pronounparlardormir
joparlavadormia
tuparlavesdormies
ell/ellaparlavadormia
nosaltresparlàvemdormíem
vosaltresparlàveudormíeu
ells/ellesparlavendormien
u5.block.4: Key irregulars: ser → era, fer → feia, veure → veia, dir → deia. Markers: abans before/used to, sempre always, quan era petit/a when I was little, cada dia every day.
u5.block.5:
Quan era petita — When I was little

— Quan eres petita, on vivies? When you were little, where did you live?

— Vivia a un poble petit. Cada estiu anàvem a la platja. I lived in a small village. Every summer we went to the beach.

— Que bé! Jo sempre jugava al carrer amb els amics. How nice! I always used to play in the street with friends.
u5.block.6: Resources for Unit 5 Conjugate the imperfet: → verbs.cat/en/practise · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat

ex.5.3.i2.html: When she was little, she played in the street.

ex.5.6.i1.html: Quan era petit, ___ jugava al carrer.

u6.title: Telling a Story — past tenses together
u6.block.0: By the end you can: tell a story in the past — using the imperfet for the background and the perifràstic/perfet for the events. This is the core A2 narrative skill.
u6.block.2: Use the imperfet for the scene, description and ongoing actions (plovia it was raining, era tard it was late); use the passat perifràstic for the things that happened (vaig sortir I went out). Mentre while + imperfet sets up a scene that an event interrupts.
u6.block.3: Narration connectors: primer first, després then/after, llavors then, de sobte suddenly, al final in the end.
u6.block.4:
Una anècdota — An anecdote

— Ahir, mentre dormia, va sonar el telèfon a les tres de la matinada! Yesterday, while I was sleeping, the phone rang at 3 a.m.!

— Quina por! Qui era? How scary! Who was it?

— Era la meva germana. Es va equivocar de número. Al final no vaig poder dormir més. It was my sister. She had the wrong number. In the end I couldn't sleep any more.
u6.block.5: Resources for Unit 6 Past tenses in context: Easy Catalan stories → youtube.com/c/EasyCatalan · Drill: → talkpal.ai

ex.6.2.i1.html: When we were little, we lived in a village.
ex.6.2.i2.html: Yesterday it was raining and I stayed home.
ex.6.3.title: True or false?
ex.6.3.i0.html: The imperfet sets the scene and background.
ex.6.3.i1.html: The perifràstic narrates the events that happened.
ex.6.3.i2.html: "mentre" is normally followed by the perifràstic.

u7.title: Weak Pronouns
u7.block.0: By the end you can: avoid repeating yourself by replacing objects with weak (clitic) pronouns — a hallmark of natural A2 Catalan.
u7.block.2:
PronounReplacesExample
el / la / els / lesdirect objectVeus el llibre? Sí, el veig.
li / elsindirect object (to him/her/them)Dono el llibre a la Maria → Li dono el llibre.
enof it / some / from thereTens pa? Sí, en tinc.
hithere / to itVas a Girona? Sí, hi vaig.
u7.block.3: Position: before the conjugated verb (el veig), but attached to an infinitive or imperative (vull veure'l, mira'l!). Apostrophe before a vowel: l'agafo, m'ajuda.
u7.block.4:
Al mercat — At the market

— Vols taronges? Do you want oranges?

— Sí, en vull un quilo. I tens pomes? Yes, I want a kilo (of them). And do you have apples?

— Sí, les tinc aquí. Les vols? Yes, I have them here. Do you want them?
u7.block.5: Resources for Unit 7 Pronouns explained + drills: → talkpal.ai · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat

u8.title: Combined Pronouns
u8.block.0: By the end you can: use two weak pronouns together (indirect + direct) — the natural way to answer "Do you give it to him?" in Catalan.
u8.block.2:
CombinationFormExample
em + el/lame'l / me laEm portes el cafè? → Me'l portes?
et + el/late'l / te laEt dono el regal → Te'l dono.
li + el/lal'hiDono el llibre a en Pau → L'hi dono.
els + elels hiDono les claus als veïns → Els les dono.
en + hi (haver-hi)n'hi haHi ha pa? Sí, n'hi ha.
u8.block.3: Position: before the conjugated verb (me'l dones), or attached to an imperative/infinitive (dona-me'l!, vols donar-me'l?).
u8.block.4:
Em deixes…? — Will you lend me…?

— Em deixes el llibre? Will you lend me the book?

— Sí, te'l deixo demà. Yes, I'll lend it to you tomorrow.

— I el diccionari, el tens? And the dictionary, do you have it?

— No n'hi ha cap a casa, ho sento. There isn't one at home, sorry.
u8.block.5: Resources for Unit 8 Combined pronouns (pas a pas): → talkpal.ai · CPNL grammar: → cpnl.cat/gramatica
ex.8.1.title: Combine the pronouns (te'l, l'hi, n'hi…).

ex.8.1.i2.html: Hi ha llet? Sí, ___ ha.

ex.8.2.i0.html: Dono el llibre a la Maria. →

ex.8.3.i1.html: Combined pronouns can't attach to an imperative.

ex.8.5.i2.html: Em portes el got? Sí, ___ porto.
ex.8.6.title: Reorder into a correct sentence.
ex.8.7.title: Listen and write what you hear, in English.
ex.8.7.i0.ans: I'll give it to you now
ex.8.8.title: Listen and write the Catalan you hear.
ex.8.9.title: Answer using a combined pronoun: Em deixes el teu boli? Dones els deures al professor? Hi ha aigua a la nevera?
u9.title: Plans & Possibilities — future & conditional
u9.block.0: By the end you can: talk about future plans and predictions, and make polite requests — "I'll go", "I would like…", "could you…?".
u9.block.2: Add the endings to the infinitive: -é, -às, -à, -em, -eu, -an. parlar → parlaré, parlaràs, parlarà, parlarem, parlareu, parlaran.
u9.block.3: Irregular stems: tenir → tindré, venir → vindré, fer → faré, poder → podré, voler → voldré, saber → sabré.
u9.block.5: Same stems + -ia, -ies, -ia, -íem, -íeu, -ien: parlaria. Used for politeness and "would": m'agradaria I would like, voldria I'd like, podries…? could you…?.
u9.block.6:
Fem plans — Making plans

— Què faràs aquest cap de setmana? What will you do this weekend?

— Aniré a la muntanya. M'agradaria descansar una mica. I'll go to the mountains. I'd like to rest a bit.

— Que bé! Podries portar-me, si vas amb cotxe? Nice! Could you take me, if you go by car?
u9.block.7: Resources for Unit 9 Conjugate futur & condicional: → verbs.cat/en/practise · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat
ex.9.1.title: Conjugate parlar in the future.

ex.9.3.i1.html: Next week we will go to Barcelona.
ex.9.4.title: True or false?

ex.9.4.i2.html: The conditional is used for polite requests.

u10.title: Telling People What to Do — the imperative
u10.block.0: By the end you can: give instructions, directions and advice — follow a recipe, tell someone the way, ask politely.
u10.block.2: Affirmative (tu / vostè / vosaltres): parla / parli / parleu. The negative uses the subjunctive: no parlis don't speak, no facis don't do.
u10.block.3: Common irregular (tu): anar → ves, fer → fes, venir → vine, dir → digues, ser → sigues. Pronouns attach: escolta'm listen to me, dona-me'l give it to me.
u10.block.5:
gira a la dreta / esquerra/ˈʒiɾə/turn right / left
tot recte/ˈtot ˈrɛktə/straight on
segueix / agafa/səˈɣeʃ/ /əˈɣafə/continue / take
u10.block.6:
Per anar a l'estació? — How do I get to the station?

— Perdoni, per anar a l'estació? Excuse me, how do I get to the station?

— Vagi tot recte, giri a la dreta i agafi el primer carrer. Go straight on, turn right and take the first street.

— Moltes gràcies! Thank you very much!
u10.block.7: Resources for Unit 10 Imperative + pronouns: → verbs.cat/en/practise · Directions vocab: Loecsen → loecsen.com
ex.10.1.title: Complete in the imperative (tu).
ex.10.2.title: Translate into Catalan (tu imperative).
ex.10.3.title: True or false?
ex.10.3.i0.html: The negative imperative uses the subjunctive.
ex.10.3.i2.html: Pronouns never attach to the imperative.
ex.10.4.title: Match the verb to its imperative (tu).
ex.10.5.title: Make it a negative imperative (tu).

ex.10.9.title: Give directions (4 steps) from your home to the nearest shop, using imperatives.
u11.title: Daily Life & Routines
u11.block.0: By the end you can: describe your daily routine with reflexive verbs and say how often you do things — a frequent A2 speaking topic.
u11.block.2: Many daily-routine verbs are reflexive — the action falls on yourself, so they take a pronoun (em, et, es, ens, us, es): llevar-se to get up → em llevo, et lleves, es lleva, ens llevem, us lleveu, es lleven.
u11.block.3:
despertar-se / llevar-se/dəspəɾˈtaɾsə/to wake up / get up
dutxar-se / rentar-se/duˈtʃaɾsə/to shower / wash
vestir-se / adormir-se/bəsˈtiɾsə/to get dressed / fall asleep
u11.block.4: Frequency: sempre always, normalment usually, sovint often, de vegades sometimes, mai never. Times: al matí / al migdia / al vespre / a la nit.
u11.block.5:
Un dia normal — A normal day

— A quina hora et lleves? What time do you get up?

— Em llevo a les set, em dutxo i esmorzo. Normalment començo a treballar a les nou. I get up at seven, shower and have breakfast. I usually start work at nine.

— I a la nit? — Sopo aviat i m'adormo de seguida! And at night? — I have dinner early and fall asleep right away!
u11.block.6: Resources for Unit 11 Daily routine vocab + audio: LingoHut → lingohut.com · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat
ex.11.1.title: Conjugate llevar-se in the present (with the reflexive pronoun).
ex.11.2.title: Complete with the reflexive verb in the present.
ex.11.2.i2.html: Nosaltres ___ tard els caps de setmana (despertar-se).

ex.11.3.i0.html: I get up at seven and shower.
ex.11.4.title: True or false?

ex.11.6.i1.html: ___ sopo fora, potser un cop al mes.
ex.11.6.i2.html: No bec cafè ___; no m'agrada.

u12.title: Health & the Body
u12.block.0: By the end you can: say what hurts, describe symptoms at the doctor's or pharmacy, and give simple advice.
u12.block.2: Use fer mal: Em fa mal el cap My head hurts, Em fan mal els peus My feet hurt. Symptoms with tenir: tenir febre / tos / mal de panxa / un refredat.
u12.block.3:
el cap / la gola/ˈkap/ /ˈɣɔlə/head / throat
l'esquena / la panxa/əsˈkenə/ /ˈpaɲʃə/back / belly
el braç / la cama / el peu/ˈbɾas/ /ˈkamə/ /ˈpɛw/arm / leg / foot
u12.block.4: Advice: hauries de descansar you should rest, pren aquesta pastilla take this pill, ves al metge go to the doctor.
u12.block.5:
A la farmàcia — At the pharmacy

— Bon dia, em fa mal la gola i tinc una mica de febre. Hello, my throat hurts and I have a slight fever.

— Hauries de descansar. Pren aquestes pastilles i beu molta aigua. You should rest. Take these pills and drink plenty of water.

— Gràcies. I si no milloro? — Doncs ves al metge. Thanks. And if I don't get better? — Then go to the doctor.
u12.block.6: Resources for Unit 12 Body & health vocab + audio: LingoHut → lingohut.com · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat

u13.title: Work & Studies
u13.block.0: By the end you can: talk about jobs and studies, manage a simple phone call, and use the formal register.
u13.block.2: Many occupations have a feminine form: metge → metgessa, professor → professora, cambrer → cambrera, dependent → dependenta, enginyer → enginyera.
u13.block.3:
treballar / estudiar/tɾəβəˈʎa/ /əstuðiˈa/to work / study
l'oficina / la botiga / l'hospital/ufiˈsinə/ /buˈtiɣə/office / shop / hospital
la carrera / la universitat/kəˈrɛɾə/degree / university
u13.block.4: Relatives: que (things & people, subject/object) — la persona que treballa aquí; qui (people, after a preposition) — la dona amb qui parlo. Phone: Digui? Hello?, De part de qui? Who's calling?, Un moment.
u13.block.5:
Una trucada — A phone call

— Digui? Hello?

— Bon dia, voldria parlar amb la senyora Roca, si us plau. Good morning, I'd like to speak with Mrs Roca, please.

— De part de qui? — Un moment, ara es posa. Who's calling? — One moment, she's coming.

u14.title: Shopping, Services & the City
u14.block.0: By the end you can: shop, ask prices and quantities, pay, and say where things are in town.
u14.block.2:
la fruiteria / la carnisseria/fɾujtəˈɾiə/greengrocer / butcher
el forn / la farmàcia/ˈfoɾn/ /fəɾˈmasiə/bakery / pharmacy
un quilo / mig quilo / una dotzena/ˈkilu/a kilo / half a kilo / a dozen
Quant és? / Quant val?/ˈkwan ˈes/How much is it?
en efectiu / amb targeta/əmb təɾˈʒɛtə/cash / by card
u14.block.3: Place: a prop de near, lluny de far from, al costat de next to, davant de in front of, darrere de behind.
u14.block.4:
A la fruiteria — At the greengrocer's

— Bon dia, què volia? Good morning, what would you like?

— Voldria un quilo de pomes i mitja dotzena d'ous. Quant és? I'd like a kilo of apples and half a dozen eggs. How much is it?

— Són quatre euros. Alguna cosa més? — No, gràcies, pago amb targeta. That's four euros. Anything else? — No thanks, I'll pay by card.
u14.block.5: Resources for Unit 14 Shopping & food vocab + audio: LingoHut → lingohut.com · Free course: Parla.cat → parla.cat

u15.title: Opinions & Getting Along
u15.block.0: By the end you can: give your opinion, agree and disagree, make suggestions, apologise and plan something with someone — the heart of the A2 speaking test.
u15.block.2: Opinion: Crec que… I think…, Em sembla que… It seems to me…, Per mi… For me…. Agreement: Estic d'acord / No estic d'acord, Tens raó you're right. Suggestions: Per què no…?, Podríem…, Què et sembla si…?. Apologies: Perdona / Ho sento / Disculpa.
u15.block.3: A peek ahead: after vull que / espero que, Catalan uses the subjunctive (Vull que vinguis I want you to come). At A2 you only need to recognise it — you'll master it at B1.
u15.block.4:
Fem un pla — Making a plan

— Què et sembla si anem al cinema dissabte? How about going to the cinema on Saturday?

— Bona idea! Però per què no sopem abans? Good idea! But why don't we have dinner first?

— D'acord, em sembla perfecte. Crec que fan una pel·lícula catalana. OK, sounds perfect. I think they're showing a Catalan film.

mock.introNote: Do all four papers in one sitting (about 90 minutes). Time yourself: Listening 30 min, Reading 45 min, Writing 30 min, Speaking 15 min. Check your answers only at the end.
mock.p2notice: El Centre Cívic ofereix cursos de català per a adults. Les classes són els dilluns i els dimecres, de 18 a 20 h. La inscripció és gratuïta fins al 30 de setembre. Cal portar el DNI.
mock.p3bTask: Task B (≈50 words) — write a short message to a friend telling them what you did last weekend and what you'll do next weekend (use a past tense and the future).
mock.p3bModel: Model: El cap de setmana passat vaig anar a Girona amb uns amics. Vam visitar la catedral i vam dinar en un restaurant del centre. A la tarda plovia, així que vam anar a un museu. M'ho vaig passar molt bé. Aquest cap de setmana que ve faré una excursió a la muntanya.
mock.p4role: Part 2 — role-play: you're at the greengrocer's. Greet the shopkeeper, ask for two items with quantities, ask the price, and pay.
mock.p4mark: Self-marking guide: give yourself a pass if you answered all eight questions in Catalan in full sentences and completed the role-play, even with small errors. Record it in the mock-exam tracker.
mock.p4model: Model: Em dic… i soc de… Ara visc a… Treballo en… / Estudio… El cap de setmana passat vaig anar a… Aquest estiu aniré a… La meva família és petita/gran… Tinc aficions com… Estudio català perquè vull viure a Andorra.
mock.p1.0: La Marta va anar a Girona amb cotxe.
mock.p1.1: Al matí va visitar la catedral.
mock.p1.5: Van tornar a casa a les vuit.
mock.p4q.0: Com et dius i d'on ets?
mock.p4q.3: Què vas fer el cap de setmana passat?
